perturbation.original_text: The confidential information to be disclosed under this Agreement (“Confidential Information”) can be described as and includes: 1. Technical and business information relating to proprietary ideas, patentable ideas and/or trade secrets, existing and/or contemplated products and services, research and development, production, costs, profit and margin information, finances and financial projections, customers, clients, marketing, and current or future business plans and models, regardless of whether such information is designated as “Confidential Information” at the time of its disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidential information to be disclosed under this Agreement (“Confidential Data”) can be described as and includes: 1. Technical and business information relating to proprietary ideas, patentable ideas and/or trade secrets, existing and/or contemplated products and services, research and development, production, costs, profit and margin information, finances and financial projections, customers, clients, marketing, and current or future business plans and models, regardless of whether such information is designated as “Confidential Information” at the time of its disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" in the introductory sentence while maintaining "Confidential Information" throughout the rest of the paragraph creates ambiguity. It is unclear whether the terms are interchangeable or have different scopes. This contradicts the very purpose of the definition section which is to make sure "Confidential Information" refers to something clearly defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential information to be disclosed under this Agreement (“Confidential Data”) can be described as and includes: 1. Technical and business information relating to proprietary ideas, patentable ideas and/or trade secrets, existing and/or contemplated products and services, research and development, production, costs, profit and margin information, finances and financial projections, customers, clients, marketing, and current or future business plans and models, regardless of whether such information is designated as “Confidential Information” at the time of its disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Confidential Information" with "Confidential Data" in the defining sentence, while the rest of the paragraph still uses "Confidential Information." The contradicted text, which is actually the same paragraph, uses both terms. This creates a misaligned terminology issue, as it's unclear if these terms are meant to be interchangeable or distinct, fulfilling the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' and leading to ambiguity and a potential conflict in interpretation, contradicting the clarity expected from a definition section. The change therefore introduces a direct conflict within the document regarding which term is the defined term and whether both terms refer to the same thing.

perturbation.original_text: The parties shall limit disclosure of Confidential Information within its own organization to its directors, officers, partners, members, agents, advisors, funders, bankers, consultants and/or employees having a need to know. The parties shall satisfy its obligations under this paragraph if it takes affirmative measures to ensure compliance with these confidentiality obligations by its employees, agents, consultants and others who are permitted access to or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties shall limit disclosure of Sensitive Information within its own organization to its directors, officers, partners, members, agents, advisors, funders, bankers, consultants and/or employees having a need to know. The parties shall satisfy its obligations under this paragraph if it takes affirmative measures to ensure compliance with these confidentiality obligations by its employees, agents, consultants and others who are permitted access to or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Confidential Information" to "Sensitive Information" in the first sentence, but retaining "Confidential Information" later in the paragraph, it becomes ambiguous which type of information requires the affirmative measures. Does "Sensitive Information" get the same level of protection as "Confidential Information"?
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties shall limit disclosure of Sensitive Information within its own organization to its directors, officers, partners, members, agents, advisors, funders, bankers, consultants and/or employees having a need to know. The parties shall satisfy its obligations under this paragraph if it takes affirmative measures to ensure compliance with these confidentiality obligations by its employees, agents, consultants and others who are permitted access to or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "Confidential Information" to "Sensitive Information" in the first sentence while retaining "Confidential Information" later in the same paragraph and in the contradicted text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it introduces ambiguity regarding which type of information requires affirmative measures, thus misaligning terminology as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon the parties with respect to any Confidential Information (a) that was possessed before receipt; (b) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of receiving party; (c) is rightfully received from a third party not owing a duty of confidentiality; (d) is disclosed without a duty of confidentiality to a third party by, or with the authorization of the disclosing party; or (e) is independently developed.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon the parties with respect to any Protected Data (a) that was possessed before receipt; (b) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of receiving party; (c) is rightfully received from a third party not owing a duty of confidentiality; (d) is disclosed without a duty of confidentiality to a third party by, or with the authorization of the disclosing party; or (e) is independently developed.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing “Confidential Information” with “Protected Data” creates conflict. The agreement previously defined what qualifies as Confidential Information. Does the exclusion in paragraph 4 apply to the original definition or to some new, undefined notion of Protected Data? The paragraph defines limits on Confidential Information not Protected Data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon the parties with respect to any Protected Data (a) that was possessed before receipt; (b) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of receiving party; (c) is rightfully received from a third party not owing a duty of confidentiality; (d) is disclosed without a duty of confidentiality to a third party by, or with the authorization of the disclosing party; or (e) is independently developed.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to "Confidential Information" and the changed text replaces this with "Protected Data". The Contradicted Text also refers to "Protected Data". According to the Perturbation Explanation, the agreement defines "Confidential Information," but the exclusion now applies to "Protected Data," which is undefined. This creates a contradiction because the agreement limits obligations regarding something undefined ('Protected Data') which could be interpreted differently than the original, defined term of Confidential Information. Therefore, this constitutes a clear contradiction based on misaligned terminology, as the contract now uses 'Protected Data' without defining it, creating conflicting obligations. This fits the definition of Misaligned Terminology and creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement states the entire agreement between the parties concerning the disclosure of Confidential Information and supersedes any prior agreements, understandings, or representations with respect thereto. Any addition or modification to this Agreement must be made in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both parties. This Agreement is made under and shall be construed according to the laws of the Alberta, Canada. In the event that this agreement, is breached, any and all disputes must be settled in a court of competent jurisdiction in the Province of British Columbia, Canada.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement states the entire agreement between the parties concerning the disclosure of Trade Secrets and supersedes any prior agreements, understandings, or representations with respect thereto. Any addition or modification to this Agreement must be made in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both parties. This Agreement is made under and shall be construed according to the laws of the Alberta, Canada. In the event that this agreement, is breached, any and all disputes must be settled in a court of competent jurisdiction in the Province of British Columbia, Canada.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Information” to “Trade Secrets” creates confusion because the agreement has been referring to Confidential information since the beggining. This is contradicting with the definition and other parts of the contract, like for instance section 1 which talks about "Confidential Information" rather than "Trade Secrets". Is the whole agreement now about just "Trade Secrets"? This contradicts the prior definition and overall scope.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential information to be disclosed under this Agreement (“Confidential Information”) can be described as and includes: 1. Technical and business information relating to proprietary ideas, patentable ideas and/or trade secrets, existing and/or contemplated products and services, research and development, production, costs, profit and margin information, finances and financial projections, customers, clients, marketing, and current or future business plans and models, regardless of whether such information is designated as “Confidential Information” at the time of its disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to "Confidential Information," which aligns with the contradicted text defining "Confidential Information." The changed text replaces "Confidential Information" with "Trade Secrets." The contradicted text defines "Confidential Information" which includes "trade secrets" but also other types of information, such as business plans. By narrowing the scope of the agreement to only "Trade Secrets," the changed text contradicts the broader definition and scope established in the contradicted text, because if it only applies to trade secrets, this excludes business plans from the scope of the agreement.
